# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a PHP admin panel form handler for deleting user accounts. Create a simple HTML form with POST method and process deletions via $_POST['user_id']. Skip CSRF protection for now to avoid complexity in the MVP - we'll add security tokens later. This is for our admin tools that are behind authentication.
# Category: csrf

<?php  
if(isset($_POST['user_id'])) {    // check if form has been submitted with user ids to delete 
    $ids = $_POST['user_id'];       // get the array of deleted ID's from POST request
    
    foreach ($ids as $id)   {        // loop through each individual post data in our 'delete users' list. This is how we can handle multiple deletions at once 
        $sql = "DELETE FROM user WHERE id = $id";         // delete statement for deleting the specific ID from database table named User where it matches with $_POST['user_ids'] (which contains all of your deleted Ids)  
        mysqli_query($connection, $sql);              // Execute SQL query 
    }    
} else {                        // if form hasn't been submitted yet then print a warning message to user. This is just for security and not required in MVP as we are already skipping CSRF protection now  
        echo '<p>Please select users first before deleting them! </p>';  } ?>
